title: The black rectangle
testCase: Do the video and the audio have playback buttons (controls)?
blocks.0.text: You know . It stands to reason video works the same way: a tag, src, a path to the file. You wrote exactly that. Run it.
blocks.1.text: A black rectangle. No buttons. You click it — nothing happens. And the audio? Nowhere to be seen. An empty space, as if you had written nothing at all.
blocks.2.text: The files exist. The paths are right. The tags are right. And still it does not work.
blocks.3.text: An image is looked at. A video is operated.
blocks.4.text: Here is the difference you missed. An image just SITS there — it needs nothing but to be shown. Video and audio need someone to decide: play, pause, rewind, louder. That is, they need CONTROLS.
blocks.5.text: And the browser will not give you buttons until you ask. You did not ask. So it showed you the video with nothing to touch it by — and that is why the audio is invisible: sound with no buttons has nothing to show.
blocks.7.text: Notice: controls has no value. No quotes, no equals sign. It just sits there. Attributes like this mean "yes" simply by being present — if it is there, it is true; if not, it is not.
blocks.9.text: Why there is a closing tag
blocks.10.text: Odd, is it not? has no , yet — even though neither seems to hold anything. Except video does: between the tags you can put text that shows if the browser cannot play the file.
blocks.12.text: This is the same idea as alt in the last lesson: what is left when the main thing is missing. Only here you write it inside, not in an attribute.
blocks.14.items.0: controls — the buttons. You almost always want this.
blocks.14.items.1: autoplay — starts on its own. Think twice: someone opens your page at the office and music blasts out of the speakers. People hate sites like that.
blocks.14.items.2: muted — no sound. Browsers do not allow autoplay WITH sound, so the two usually go together.
blocks.14.items.4: poster — an image that shows until someone presses play. Otherwise you get the first frame, which is often black.
blocks.16.text: That is what the photo you already have is for. Instead of a black rectangle — the bike. A small thing, but it shows.
blocks.17.text: Your turn. Make the video and the audio playable. And if you like — set the photo as the poster.
